descript.abstract: Depicts structural range improvements that are points. This will include handling facilities and Range Water System components like source, storage and pumps. These improvements are assets tracking expenditures on the ground across the landscape.
descript.purpose: The data was developed to fill a need for an internal and external spatial representation of the point structural improvements used in the management of the Rangeland resource. The data will be used to create permit maps in addition to field working maps and any other logical uses that the internal and external public may determine this information is helpful to.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

dataqual.logic: Data stewards have been relied on to validate the data quality and accuracy of the point structural improvements used in the management of the rangeland resource. Handling Facilities and water system components have been identified using various methods through the years like GPS or digitizing. Spatial data comes from the district and is consolidated at the forest level. This will be a consolidated national extent of the data entered at the lowest level of the agency. Data quality is dependent on quality data maintenance by local data stewards.
dataqual.complete: This data is current only as of the date of publication and may change daily. The need for a daily refresh is key to incorporate any and all local changes as they are made and keep the data in EDW fresh.

horizpa.horizpar: Positional accuracy is veryified by visual comparison to source datasets

procstep.procdesc: Data is the result of a national data call where local units were asked to identify and provide spatial and tabular data to represent linear structural improvements like fences and pipelines. Local units are to follow the current GIS Data Dictionaries and load their local data into the appropriate SDE to be collected and merged into a national extent.

enttyp.enttypd: Feature class: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The numeric code, six digits, for the Admin Organizational unit (RRFFDD where R = Region code number, F = Forest code number, and D = District code number). The administrative organization is the Ranger District on which the feature is located.
The name associated with the Admin Organization identified.

The numeric code for the Managing Organizational unit (RRFFDD where R = Region code number, F = Forest code number, and D = District code number). The managing organization is the Ranger District that manages the feature, regardless of the location of the feature.
The name associated with the Managing Organization identified.
Globally unique identifier across all Forest Service databases, foreign key to most Forest Service National Application databases. Specifically for improvements.
A number that uniquely identifies the improvement on the Forest.
The name of the feature. The name need not be unique.
The type of feature.

edom.edomvd: Feature is a set of MULTIPLE PENS

attr.attrdef: Condition and state of repair of the feature.

edom.edomvd: Feature should be or has been removed.

attr.attrdef: The date the feature was last inspected.
Globally unique identifier across all Forest Service databases, foreign key to most Forest Service National Application databases. Specifically for allotments.
The name of the allotment. The name need not be unique.
A 5 character alpha numeric identifier assigned to the allotment.

Globally unique identifier across all Forest Service databases, foreign key to most Forest Service National Application databases. Specifically for the parent record associated with an improvement like the Range Water System that a trough is part of.
An alpha numeric identifier that uniquely identifies the parent feature ID.
The name of the parent feature. The name need not be unique.
The type of feature.

edom.edomvd: The parent feature type is a HANDLING FACILITY. A handling facility is a structural improvement used to aid in the management or movement of livestock.
The parent feature is a RANGE WATER SYSTEM. A range water system is a grouping of individual components of a single system.

attr.attrdef: Globally unique identifier across all Forest Service databases, foreign key to most Forest Service National Application databases.
An alpha numeric identifier for the permit within the Admin Org.

edom.edomvd: The permit is within the time frame for which it was issued.
All lines of permitted use have expired, been cancelled or been waived.

attr.attrdef: Globally unique identifier across all Forest Service databases, foreign key to most Forest Service National Application databases.
An alpha numeric identifier for the permittee.

Revision date, the date and time when the feature was created or last updated. Time is entered using a 24-hour clock (military time) based on the Pacific Standard Time (PST) zone. Tasks associated with each revision should be documented in the process steps of theme-based metadata. Entering the date only is acceptable.
Feature data source code that describes how the data feature was derived or collected.
Distance (+/-), in dataset distance units, of the expected difference between feature and real-world object locations.
A type of UUID (Universal Unique Identifier) in which values are automatically assigned by the geodatabase when a row is created.

attrdomv.udom: A type of UUID (Universal Unique Identifier) in which values are automatically assigned by the geodatabase when a row is created.

attr.attrdef: Feature geometry.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
